name: Conservation of linear momentum
conventions: p_i = m_i v_i is the linear momentum vector of particle i
vector equation: each component (x, y, z) is independently conserved when net external force vanishes along that direction
applies to a closed system: total external force on the system is zero (or sum of external impulses negligible over the interaction)
valid in any inertial reference frame; values differ between frames but conservation holds in each
relativistic generalisation: p = gamma m v with gamma = 1/sqrt(1 - v^2/c^2) — same conservation statement applies
expectedLimits: elastic collision: total momentum AND total kinetic energy conserved
inelastic collision: total momentum conserved, kinetic energy not conserved (some converted to heat / deformation)
perfectly inelastic collision: bodies stick together; final velocity v_f = (sum m_i v_i) / (sum m_i)
explosion / fragmentation: a single body splits — total momentum of fragments equals initial momentum
centre-of-mass frame: total momentum is zero by definition; collisions look symmetric
external force present: dp/dt = F_ext (Newton's 2nd law for the centre of mass) — momentum changes at the rate of impulse delivered
references: Newton (1687) — Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica, Book I
Halliday, Resnick & Walker — Fundamentals of Physics, ch.9
Goldstein, Poole & Safko — Classical Mechanics, ch.1
